candidates.title: Sask. Roughrider in fatal crash was driving on wrong side of divided highway: RCMP
candidates.description: Saskatchewan Roughriders linebacker Jayden Dalke was driving the wrong way on the highway with alcohol and cannabis in his vehicle before the crash that killed him and another man, RCMP say.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Alberta, Saskatchewan offer 'most compelling' case for U.S. oil supply: Hoekstra
candidates.description: U.S. needs to find three to four million more barrels of oil per day over the next decade, and Canada can be one of the best places to get it, says Pete Hoekstra.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Alberta government gives public libraries Jan. 1 deadline to comply with new restrictions
candidates.description: The province informed public libraries this month that they have until the start of the new year to establish new practices restricting access to library materials containing sexually explicit images to anyone 15 years old or younger, without authorization from a parent or guardian.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Amid wider statue debate, Gatineau quietly reinstalls monument to Samuel de Champlain
candidates.description: The City of Gatineau has quietly reinstalled a statue of 17th-century explorer and Quebec City founder Samuel de Champlain, nearly a decade after it was removed for maintenance.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: She needs $12K-a-month drug to treat incurable cancer, but insurance won’t pay
candidates.description: Sophie Djeme-Mi Koumazock’s doctor prescribed her a new drug to slow the progression of incurable breast cancer. But as a federal employee, her insurance plan through Canada Life won’t foot the monthly bill, which could be as expensive as $12,000.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: What did Vancouver get from the World Cup?
candidates.description: For five weeks, the FIFA Men's World Cup brought big crowds, busy transit and new energy to downtown Vancouver. Now that the crowds have left, the city is weighing the tournament’s legacy, with questions about public costs, Granville Street and whether soccer momentum can be sustained in B.C.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: TIFF turns the spotlight on Canada in its gala and special presentations
candidates.description: The 51st Toronto International Film Festival announced its Gala and Special Presentations lineups, which include Chris Rock’s Misty Green and Peter Farrelly’s I Play Rocky, alongside major Canadian premieres and closing-night gala Villeneuve: The Rise of a Legend.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Why these Albertans are concerned about the environmental impacts of a proposed C02 pipeline
candidates.description: Advocates, researchers and industry experts weigh in on the proposed Pathways Project, which aims to capture and store carbon dioxide emissions underground.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Unifor members ratify new deal with Ford Motor that includes wage increases, new investment
candidates.description: The union representing more than 5,000 workers at the Ford Motor Company of Canada has ratified a new three-year collective agreement — including a wage increase of three per cent per year; renewal of a cost-of-living allowance (COLA) and $1.2 billion in planned investments in Canadian manufacturing, according to details released by both the union and the company.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Canada's inflation rate eased to 2.8% in June thanks to lower gas prices
candidates.description: While higher oil prices have sent the cost of gas up in recent months, the recent ceasefire in the Middle East led to a 10.2 per cent drop in the cost of gas month-over-month. Statistics Canada says inflation was unchanged from May to June when gas prices are taken out of the equation.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: CBC Sports reveals Commonwealth Games broadcast team and coverage details
candidates.description: CBC Sports will begin its coverage of the Commonwealth Games on Thursday, with the opening ceremony from Glasgow at 2:30 p.m., live on CBC-TV and streamed at CBCSports.ca and CBC Gem.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Erica Wiebe's Commonwealth Games journey comes full circle at Glasgow 2026
candidates.description: Erica Wiebe’s first taste of the Commonwealth Games experience came back in 2014 in Glasgow. Now, 12 years later, the two-time Commonwealth wrestling gold medallist and Olympic champion is back at the Games in the Scottish city, but in a much different role: chef de mission for Canada’s team.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Proposed AI data centre hits close to home for concerned residents in Wheatland County, Alberta
candidates.description: Diode Ventures is seeking approval to develop an AI data centre campus in the southwest corner of Wheatland County. County administration says the project could generate significant tax revenue, while residents are raising concerns over local impact.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Inquest begins into death of man stunned, pepper sprayed and beaten during arrest
candidates.description: The five-day public inquest into the death of Boden Umpherville begins at King's Bench Court in Prince Albert on Monday.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Crown won't prosecute some charges laid in Project South police corruption investigation
candidates.description: Superior court indictments filed for eight of those accused in the police corruption investigation known as Project South list 33 fewer charges against them than what they were originally charged with by police earlier this year. Screening and streamlining charges in this way is a normal part of the prosecution process to ensure the Crown pursues the best case it can, according to current and former prosecutors.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Saint John man stabbed his own sons to death, altered publication ban reveals
candidates.description: Publication bans still prohibit naming the two Saint John boys Roman Kamyshnyy was convicted earlier this month of murdering, but CBC News can now report all of the relationships involved and tell more fully this tragic story of domestic violence the Crown described as being “driven by vengeance.”
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Government tip-off prompted spy agency to look into federal scientist, CSIS says
candidates.description: The country's top spy agency says Natural Resources Canada approached it with concerns about former federal scientist Dennis Lu more than two decades ago — not the other way around — and has made a rare disclosure of one of its documents in court to set the record straight, CBC News has learned.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Roughriders linebacker and captain Jayden Dalke killed in crash
candidates.description: The Saskatchewan Roughriders are mourning linebacker and team captain Jayden Dalke, who died in a car crash Saturday evening. The team announced his death in a statement Sunday.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Who is Nolan Wells, and why has the Black Mississippi teen's death received widespread attention?
candidates.description: The death of Nolan Wells, a Black teenager whose body was located on an island off the Mississippi coast after he spent the Fourth of July there with three white friends, has drawn widespread attention amid mounting unanswered questions.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Expansion of U.S. strikes on Iran leads to more retaliation across Persian Gulf
candidates.description: The United States conducted a new round of airstrikes early Monday targeting Iran after announcing the death of another service member. Iran responded by launching an attack targeting Bahrain, the home of the U.S. Navy's 5th Fleet, as well as Kuwait.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: 10 bodies recovered, dozens feared dead after passenger ferry sinks off coast of Guyana
candidates.description: Guyana's government said on Monday that 10 bodies had been recovered from a ferry that capsized off the coast over the weekend with 133 people on board.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Meet Jimothy, the unusually round Seattle raccoon capturing hearts worldwide
candidates.description: A Seattle raccoon with an unusually short body nicknamed Jimothy has become an internet sensation after a video of the critter scampering across a field went viral.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Russian missile strike kills 10 in Black Sea near Ukraine
candidates.description: A Russian missile strike on a ‌ship carrying corn near Ukraine's southern port of Odesa killed 10 people, Ukrainian officials said on Monday, in the deadliest attack in a weeks-long flare-up of violence in the Black Sea.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Andy Burnham officially becomes British prime minister
candidates.description: Andy Burnham became the fifth British prime minister in four years and the seventh in a decade as he formally accepted an invitation from King Charles to form a government.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: The big stories — and best photos — from the 2026 World Cup
candidates.description: Forty-eight teams, 104 matches, 39 days. Here's a look back at some of the main storylines from the FIFA World Cup 2026 — as illustrated by some of the tournament's best photos.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Trump's 'big lie' in prime time
candidates.description: Donald Trump is using the U.S. presidency to re-litigate the 2020 election. Today, we look at his latest claims and whether the president is laying the groundwork to challenge the legitimacy of the upcoming midterms.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Torres fires Spain past Argentina in extra time to win World Cup, denying Messi a 2nd title
candidates.description: Ferran Torres' first goal of this year's World Cup was the goal of his life, and it put Spain back atop the soccer world. Denying Lionel Messi a second straight title, Torres came off the bench and scored in the 106th minute and Spain ended Argentina's reign with a 1-0 win on Sunday.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: U.S. FDA says data continues to support Taylor Farms' lettuce as cyclospora source
candidates.description: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration said on Monday that its traceback investigation into a cyclosporiasis outbreak across five states continues to support Taylor Farms' lettuce ‌from Central Mexico as a product source.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: At least 6 people killed, hundreds displaced after earthquake rocks Peru
candidates.description: A 5.5-magnitude earthquake shook the Andes region of Peru, killing at least six people, authorities said Sunday. More than 30 people were injured and 300 have been displaced.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: How modern warfare is reshaping Ukraine’s natural environment
candidates.description: From Chornobyl's endangered wild horses to bird nests made of drone cables, modern warfare is reshaping fragile ecosystems along Ukraine’s front lines.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Canada to temporarily bar entry by foreign nationals recently in Congo due to Ebola risk
candidates.description: As part of new, temporary border measures ‌to combat the spread ​of Ebola, the ​Canadian government on Sunday said ​it would deny entry to ‌foreign nationals who had been to the Democratic Republic of the Congo ‌within the past 21 days.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Colourful World Cup halftime performance brings out Shakira, Madonna and Justin Bieber
candidates.description: Madonna, Justin Bieber, BTS and Shakira each brought a bit of one of their hits to the World Cup's first halftime show in a whirlwind performance Sunday that also squeezed in Burna Boy, Ted Lasso and many Muppets.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Is this the most controversial World Cup yet?
candidates.description: After more than 100 matches across Canada, Mexico and the United States, the FIFA World Cup 2026 is about to come to an end, cementing its legacy with highs, lows — and seemingly endless controversies. So, is this the most controversial World Cup? We talk to experts to get a sense of how this year's decisions have affected the tournament.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: U.K. seeks extradition after influencer Tate brothers arrested in U.S.
candidates.description: Influencer brothers Andrew and Tristan Tate were arrested by federal authorities on Saturday in Miami, according to the U.S. Marshals Service.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: What the 1976 Montreal Games represent to those who boycotted it, 50 years later
candidates.description: A young Nigerian woman lands in Montreal, full of excitement and optimism for her first Olympic Games. A Tanzanian track star never leaves home. On the 50th anniversary of the 1976 Games, both athletes reflect on the boycott that demanded they put aside their dreams for a greater cause.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Russian ballistic missile attack on Kyiv kills 1, wounds 16
candidates.description: Russia battered the Ukrainian capital of Kyiv overnight ‌with one of its biggest ballistic missile barrages of the war that began in February 2022.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Trump says US strikes hit Iran in 'honour' of American soldiers killed
candidates.description: Iran responded with attacks in Syria and Jordan, as two ships reportedly exploded in the Strait of Hormuz.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Yemen's Houthis announce 'maritime embargo' on Saudi Arabia
candidates.description: The Houthis say the embargo begins immediately and is a response to a Saudi blockade of ports and airports in Yemen.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Rare identical quadruplets born in Australia
candidates.description: The parents of the four naturally-conceived girls were taken by "immense surprise" with the pregnancy, the hospital said.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Notorious drug cartel leader Ismael Zambada sentenced to life in US prison
candidates.description: Known as "El Mayo", the co-founder the Sinaloa Cartel was also ordered to pay $15bn after he admitted to sending vast amounts of drugs to the US.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Hamas names Khalil al-Hayya as new overall leader
candidates.description: The Palestinian armed group's Qatar-based chief negotiator replaces Yahya Sinwar, who was killed by Israeli forces in Gaza in 2024.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Scores feared dead after passenger ferry capsizes off Guyana
candidates.description: Sixty-seven people have been rescued but the ferry is thought to have had more than 130 people on board.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Newcastle, Liverpool and England legend Keegan dies
candidates.description: Former England captain and manager Kevin Keegan dies at the age of 75, seven weeks after he revealed he had been diagnosed with stage four cancer.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Utah firefighter and four family members killed in flash flooding
candidates.description: A husband, wife and their three sons died - meaning that only a daughter who did not join the outing is still alive.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: 'If only people knew what a tick could do' - the deadly meat allergies caused by tick bites
candidates.description: Mammalian meat allergy is a growing problem along Australia's east coast - and it's caused by tick bites.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Norway opens new memorial for victims of 2011 massacre
candidates.description: The artwork in the capital Oslo was unveiled on Sunday ahead of the 15th anniversary of the attack.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Tate brothers arrested in US as further UK charges take total to 59
candidates.description: The Crown Prosecution Service says it has requested their extradition from the US, while their lawyer says they are innocent.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Russia launches major ballistic missile attack on Ukrainian cities
candidates.description: Eight people die and dozens are injured as several cities are hit by missile and drone attacks, while Kyiv continues its own assault on Russian infrastructure.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Test that found source of US explosive diarrhoea outbreak was false positive, health officials say
candidates.description: The latest update from the US Food and Drug Administration means the exact source of the parasite is still not known.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Neighbours actor Terence Donovan dies aged 90
candidates.description: His son, actor Jason Donovan, posted to social media that his father died on Saturday night peacefully in Melbourne, Australia
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Cuban dissident Luis Manuel Otero Alcántara goes into exile in US
candidates.description: He was arrested in 2021 during Cuba's largest anti-government protests in decades and spent five years ⁠in prison.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Why are thousands of people protesting in Delhi?
candidates.description: Thousands of mostly young people tried to march on India's parliament to demand changes to how the education system is run.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Shakira, Madonna, Justin Bieber and BTS perform at colourful World Cup half-time show
candidates.description: Robbie Williams and Jennifer Hudson also performed, with Kylie Jenner and Timothee Chalamet among the celebrities in the crowds.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: ‘All I think about is her’: Families demand answers as Nigeria's boat disasters keep happening
candidates.description: Hundreds of lives are lost each year in Nigeria as a result of boat accidents yet nothing seems to change.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Russians turn to cash, putting more strain on slowing wartime economy
candidates.description: Russians have been hit by mobile internet shutdowns and more businesses are seeking to dodge tax after more than four years of war with Ukraine.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Private jets flock to Montana - but locals can't afford the trailer park
candidates.description: Rents in Bozeman, Montana have skyrocketed as wealthy out-of-towners flock to the city to experience the American West.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: From hundreds to thousands: How India's 'cockroach' protest movement has grown
candidates.description: BBC's Azadeh Moshiri reports as thousands attempt to march to India's parliament in support of the Cockroach Janta Party's demand for education reforms.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Why are Ukraine's military recruiters facing violence in their own country?
candidates.description: The latest National Police Service report recorded more than 600 assaults on recruitment officers to date and warned that the aggression has become “increasingly threatening”.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Spanish and Argentinian fans react outside World Cup final
candidates.description: Ecstatic Spanish spectators and disappointed Argentine fans spoke to the BBC after the World Cup final on Sunday.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: AliExpress fined record €550m by EU for allowing sale of illegal goods
candidates.description: The Chinese retailer allowed unsafe toys and counterfeit clothes to be sold on its site, says the EU.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Canadian wildfires rage as Trump warns Carney again over 'poisoning' US air
candidates.description: The US president raised the issue with the the Canadian prime minister while the pair attended the World Cup Final in New Jersey.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Is Burnham promising a new dawn for North Sea oil and gas?
candidates.description: The incoming PM is expected to announce fresh support for the North Sea from day one of his premiership.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: The hunger-striking engineer demanding education reforms in India
candidates.description: Climate activist Sonam Wangchuk has a long history of challenging the status quo and refusing food to highlight his causes.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: The hidden cost of the night shift and how to sleep it off
candidates.description: More than three million people in the UK work night shifts, which can greatly impact their health. What can be done to help them?
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: India's Gen Z 'Cockroach People's Party' started as satire but is serious about change
candidates.description: They call themselves the "cockroaches" and they're on the march — demanding action against corruption, amid a shortage of opportunities for young people in India.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Putin meets North Korean foreign minister in diplomatic push
candidates.description: The visit by North Korean Foreign Minister Choe Son Hui comes without an obvious occasion, such as a multilateral diplomatic event or state anniversary, and has raised speculation that she may be coordinating a possible visit to Russia by North Korean leader Kim Jong Un.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: 3 U.S. personnel killed in Iran conflict. And, Hunter Biden opens up to NPR
candidates.description: Three U.S. service members have been killed since Friday amid rising tension between the U.S. and Iran. And, Hunter Biden talks to NPR's Newsmakers about addiction and public scrutiny.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: At least 930 people have died in Congo's Ebola outbreak
candidates.description: Congo's health ministry said the deaths were recorded out of the 2,344 cases confirmed as of Saturday. Thirty-seven new deaths were recorded in the previous 24 hours in one of the highest totals.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: U.S. and Iran launch new strikes after 3 U.S. service members are killed
candidates.description: The U.S. attacks were the ninth consecutive day of bombing since peace talks between the two countries collapsed in dispute over a memorandum of understanding focused on the Strait of Hormuz.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Andy Burnham becomes U.K. prime minister
candidates.description: In a transition of power presided over by King Charles, Andy Burnham has replaced Keir Starmer as the United Kingdom's prime minister. Both are from the center-left Labour Party.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Who is Andy Burnham, the U.K.'s new prime minister?
candidates.description: On Monday, former Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham succeeds Keir Starmer as the United Kingdom's prime minister. Burnham's politics have been shaped by England's north-south class divide.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: An Israeli paraglider tries to save migrating swifts that nest in the Western Wall
candidates.description: Swifts have nested in Jerusalem's Western Wall since biblical times. Now one Israeli paraglider is trying to save them.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Cuban artist and dissident exiled from country after 5 years in prison arrives in US
candidates.description: A famous Cuban dissident artist and musician, Luis Manuel Otero Alcántara, arrived in Miami on Saturday after being released from a five-year prison sentence on the condition that he leave his country.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Court ruling freezes Paramount-Warner Bros. merger for now
candidates.description: Paramount's owners were hoping they'd be firmly in control of rival Warner Bros. Discovery by the end of this week. Monday's temporary restraining order puts that plan on hold.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Deloitte-run systems denied Medicaid to disabled people. New laws could make it worse
candidates.description: Complex computer systems that decide whether people get Medicaid benefits have a history of errors. Now, changes to comply with Trump's tax and domestic policy law are inundating those systems.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: So distressed you can't think straight? Lower the intensity with these 4 skills
candidates.description: A clinical psychologist shares effective strategies to quickly de-escalate stress and anxiety. That includes dunking your head into a bowl of ice water to trigger your mammalian dive reflex.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: New study finds avocado oil products may contain cheaper oils
candidates.description: Sales of avocado oil and products made with it are booming as consumers willingly pay a premium, drawn by the oil's health halo. New research finds many products actually contain other, cheaper oils.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Violence repeatedly erupts in dementia care despite warnings, inspections show
candidates.description: Nursing homes and assisted living facilities can be fertile ground for violence between residents with dementia, including fatal assaults. Court records and inspecitions how safeguards fall short.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Small Craft Advisory issued July 20 at 1:22PM CDT until July 21 at 1:00AM CDT by NWS Duluth MN
candidates.description: * WHAT...West winds 10 to 15 kt with gusts up to 30 kt and waves 1 to 4 ft. * WHERE...Portions of Lake Superior. * WHEN...Until 1 AM CDT Tuesday. * IMPACTS...Conditions will be hazardous to small craft.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Special Weather Statement issued July 20 at 2:22PM EDT by NWS Greenville-Spartanburg SC
candidates.description: At 222 PM EDT, Doppler radar was tracking a strong thunderstorm 9 miles north of Brevard, or 4 miles southeast of Mount Pisgah, moving northeast at 10 mph. HAZARD...Wind gusts up to 40 mph. SOURCE...Radar indicated. IMPACT...Gusty winds could knock down tree limbs and blow around unsecured objects. Locations impacted include... Mills River, Etowah, B.R. Parkway-Mount Pisgah To The Nc Arboretum, Mount Pisgah, Looking Glass, Forge Mountain, Horse Shoe, and North Transylvania.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Special Weather Statement issued July 20 at 2:22PM EDT by NWS Wakefield VA
candidates.description: At 222 PM EDT, Doppler radar was tracking a strong thunderstorm 8 miles east of Windsor, moving southeast at 10 mph. HAZARD...Wind gusts up to 40 mph, heavy rain, and frequent lightning. SOURCE...Radar indicated. IMPACT...Gusty winds could knock down tree limbs and blow around unsecured objects. Reduced visibility and minor flooding possible. Frequent cloud to ground lightning strikes. This storm will remain over mainly rural areas of east central Bertie and southern Chowan Counties, including the following locations... Merry Hill, Edenhouse, Saint Johns, Mount Gould, Macedonia, Hancock, Midway, and Somerset.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Flood Advisory issued July 20 at 2:22PM EDT until July 20 at 4:15PM EDT by NWS Raleigh NC
candidates.description: * WHAT...Flooding caused by excessive rainfall is expected. * WHERE...Halifax and Warren counties in central North Carolina. * WHEN...Until 415 PM EDT. * IMPACTS...Minor flooding in low-lying and poor drainage areas. * ADDITIONAL DETAILS... - At 221 PM EDT, Doppler radar indicated heavy rain due to thunderstorms. Minor flooding is ongoing or expected to begin shortly in the advisory area. Between 1 and 1.5 inches of rain have fallen. - Additional rainfall amounts of 1 to 2 inches are expected over the area. This additional rain will result in minor flooding. - Some locations that will experience flooding include... Roanoke Rapids, Warrenton, Halifax, Scotland Neck, Norlina, Littleton, Enfield, Weldon, Macon, Airlie, Medoc Mountain State Park, Rosemary, Roanoke Rapids Lake, Arcola, South Weldon, Southpointe And Morningstar Marinas, Lake Gaston, Tillery and Wise. - http://www.weather.gov/safety/flood
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Special Weather Statement issued July 20 at 2:22PM EDT by NWS Newport/Morehead City NC
candidates.description: At 222 PM EDT, Doppler radar was tracking a strong thunderstorm over Warsaw, moving east at 15 mph. HAZARD...Wind gusts up to 40 mph. SOURCE...Radar indicated. IMPACT...Gusty winds could knock down tree limbs and blow around unsecured objects. Locations impacted include... Warsaw, Bowdens, Kenansville, and Sarecta.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Small Craft Advisory issued July 20 at 2:22PM EDT until July 23 at 12:00AM EDT by NWS Charleston SC
candidates.description: * WHAT...South winds 15 to 25 kt with gusts up to 30 kt and seas 3 to 6 ft expected. * WHERE...Savannah to Altamaha Sound out 20 NM. * WHEN...From 2 PM Tuesday to midnight EDT Wednesday night. * IMPACTS...Conditions will be hazardous to small craft.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Small Craft Advisory issued July 20 at 2:22PM EDT until July 23 at 12:00AM EDT by NWS Charleston SC
candidates.description: * WHAT...South winds 15 to 25 kt with gusts up to 30 kt and seas 4 to 6 ft expected. * WHERE...Savannah to Altamaha Sound from 20 to 60 NM. * WHEN...From 2 PM Tuesday to midnight EDT Wednesday night. * IMPACTS...Conditions will be hazardous to small craft.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Small Craft Advisory issued July 20 at 2:22PM EDT until July 23 at 5:00AM EDT by NWS Charleston SC
candidates.description: * WHAT...South winds 15 to 25 kt with gusts up to 30 kt and seas 3 to 6 ft expected. * WHERE...South Santee River to Edisto Beach out 20 NM. * WHEN...From noon Tuesday to 5 AM EDT Thursday. * IMPACTS...Conditions will be hazardous to small craft.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Small Craft Advisory issued July 20 at 2:22PM EDT until July 23 at 5:00AM EDT by NWS Charleston SC
candidates.description: * WHAT...South winds 15 to 25 kt with gusts up to 30 kt and seas 3 to 6 ft expected. * WHERE...Edisto Beach to Savannah out 20 NM. * WHEN...From noon Tuesday to 5 AM EDT Thursday. * IMPACTS...Conditions will be hazardous to small craft.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Special Weather Statement issued July 20 at 2:21PM EDT by NWS Tallahassee FL
candidates.description: At 221 PM EDT, Doppler radar was tracking a strong thunderstorm 7 miles south of Moultrie, moving north at 20 mph. HAZARD...Wind gusts up to 40 mph. SOURCE...Radar indicated. IMPACT...Gusty winds could knock down tree limbs and blow around unsecured objects. Locations impacted include... Moultrie, Thomasville, Quitman, Boston, Omega, Norman Park, Doerun, Ochlocknee, Pavo, Coolidge, Riverside, Schley, Morven, Berlin, Funston, Barwick, Sale City, Ellenton, Reed Bingham State Park, and Minton.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: M 5.5 - 2 km WSW of Sicaya, Peru
candidates.description: The United States Geological Survey reported an earthquake of magnitude 5.5. The event occurred near 2 km WSW of Sicaya, Peru. Official monitoring data may be updated as scientists review additional measurements.
candidates.author: U.S. Geological Survey
